title: Mini Project: Student Grade Book
description: Combine everything you've learned into a complete program.
objectives: Combine functions, lists, dictionaries, loops, and conditionals
Parse structured input and produce formatted output
recap: You combined variables, input, conditionals, loops, lists, dictionaries, and functions into a complete program. This is real programming!
practicePrompts: Add a feature to find the student with the highest average grade.
Can you save the grade book to a file and load it back?

practiceExercises.title: Find the top student
practiceExercises.prompt: Given the grade book below, write a function `top_student(book)` that returns the name of the student with the highest average. Print their name. Expected output: `Bob`.
practiceExercises.goal: Loop over a dict-of-lists, compute averages, track the max.
hints: Loop over book.items(): you get (name, grades) pairs.
Track best_name and best_avg as you go.

practiceExercises.title: Class average
practiceExercises.prompt: Given the same grade book, write a function `class_average(book)` that returns the average of ALL grades across all students, rounded to 1 decimal place. Print it. Expected output: `84.3`.
practiceExercises.goal: Flatten nested data and compute a single aggregate.
hints: Collect all grades into one list, or accumulate total + count directly.
round(value, 1) rounds to one decimal place.

practiceExercises.title: Formatted grade report
practiceExercises.prompt: Print a one-line report for Alice in this exact format: `Alice: avg=85.0, min=80, max=90`.
practiceExercises.goal: Combine dict lookup, list built-ins, and f-string formatting.
hints: Use sum()/len() for avg, min() and max() for the extremes.
